Metadata.title: The Last Line — Goalkeeper Academy
Metadata.description: Specialist goalkeeper academy in the Chablais (Aigle, Switzerland). Training, camps and coaching for keepers, from age 10 to seniors.

Footer.tagline: The goalkeeper academy of the Chablais region. Specialized training for goalkeepers, from age 10 to senior.
Footer.copyright: © {year} The Last Line. All rights reserved.

HomePage.metaDescription: The Last Line, the goalkeeper academy of the Chablais (Aigle, Switzerland). Specialist training, camps and coaching for keepers, from age 10 to seniors.

hero.title: Training tomorrow's goalkeepers
hero.subtitle: Weekly sessions, holiday camps and private coaching for goalkeepers, from age 10 to senior, in the Chablais region.
hero.imageAlt: Young goalkeeper diving during a training session in the Chablais

values.title: An approach built for the toughest position on the pitch

specialized.title: 100% goalkeepers
specialized.description: Every coach is a former keeper. No mixed sessions with outfield players. Every drill targets a specific goalkeeping skill.

progression.title: Measured progress
progression.description: Technical review every quarter, analysis and personalised goals. You see the progress, and so do your player and child.

passion.title: Fun first
passion.description: Small groups, family atmosphere, playful drills. Our goalkeepers come back because they enjoy the work.

offers.title: Find the format that fits you
offers.subtitle: Whether you're looking for weekly coaching, an intensive camp or a one-off boost, we have a format for you.

tour.title: The Tour
tour.description: A commitment over a half-season of 18 sessions, for youth and adults alike.

season.title: Full season
season.description: The whole season over 36 sessions, the best per-session rate.

camps.title: Camps
camps.description: Intensive 3- to 5-day camps during school holidays. Half-day or full-day formats.

testimonials.title: What families say

parent.quote: Within six months our son's distribution game changed completely. The team is demanding but caring, exactly what we were looking for.
parent.author: Sandrine M.

keeper.quote: I'm learning things I'd never seen at my club. And we have a lot of fun in training too.
keeper.author: Léo, age 14

club.quote: The Last Line handles the specific training of our keepers, which frees up valuable time for our team work.
club.author: Marc D.

testimonials.comingSoon: The first reviews from our families are coming very soon.

partners.title: They trust us
partners.subtitle: Partner club in the Chablais and the leading goalkeeper event in French-speaking Switzerland.

blogPreview.title: Tips, analysis and behind the scenes

post1.title: 5 drills to improve footwork on aerial claims
post1.excerpt: Timing and balance are built on the ground before they're built in the air. Here is our favorite routine.

post2.title: Conceding a goal: the 30-second reset routine
post2.excerpt: How our coaches train goalkeepers to move on instantly after a mistake.

post3.title: Easter camp 2026 wrap-up
post3.excerpt: Three days of intensity, two venues, 24 goalkeepers. The recap in pictures and key numbers.

GianaPage.metaDescription: The Giana Stop & Shoot, the French-speaking Swiss tournament entirely dedicated to goalkeepers, created by Gianluca Giannarelli, co-founder of The Last Line.

hero.title: Giana Stop & Shoot
hero.subtitle: The French-speaking Swiss event fully dedicated to goalkeepers. One day, one tournament, one shared passion.

about.title: A unique format for goalkeepers
about.intro: Each year, Giana Stop & Shoot gathers around a hundred goalkeepers from all over French-speaking Switzerland for technical contests and a knockout mini-tournament. No outfield players: just goalkeepers, their gloves and an audience of connoisseurs.

partnership.title: An event created by our co-founder

values.title: Shared values
values.description: The tournament was born from the same belief as the academy: put the goalkeeper at the centre, celebrate the position and create moments shared between enthusiasts.

coaching.title: Coaches on site
coaching.description: Our coaches run the technical workshops and support young goalkeepers throughout the day.

spotting.title: Talent spotting
spotting.description: The event also serves as a showcase to spot talents and offer them follow-up at the academy.

info.title: All the details on the official site

cta.title: Want to be there?
cta.subtitle: Join us at the Monthey stadium or register your goalkeeper via the official website.

Legal.placeholderNotice: Template wording. It must be reviewed by your legal advisor before publishing. Factual elements (legal name, address, host) need to be filled in.

imprint.metaDescription: Legal notice and editorial information for the The Last Line website.
imprint.title: Legal notice
imprint.intro: This legal notice identifies the publisher of thelastline.ch, its host and the general terms of use.

sections.heading: Publisher
sections.body: thelastline.ch is published by the non-profit sports association “The Last Line”, governed by art. 60 ff. of the Swiss Civil Code and constituted on 1 July 2026. Its seat is in Torgon, municipality of Vionnaz (canton of Valais), Switzerland. The association pursues an idealistic purpose (training goalkeepers), with no profit motive. It is represented by its co-presidents, Arthur Chazelle and Gianluca Giannarelli. Contact: contact@thelastline.ch. Business ID (IDE/UID): CHE-235.374.703.

sections.heading: Hosting
sections.body: The site is hosted by Vercel Inc., 340 S Lemon Ave #4133, Walnut, CA 91789 (USA). The database and authentication are provided by Supabase, with data stored in the European Union (Ireland). E-mail is delivered through the mail provider LWS.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
sections.body: All content on the site (text, logos, images, graphics) is protected by copyright. Any reproduction or use, in whole or in part, without the association's prior written consent is prohibited.

sections.heading: Liability
sections.body: The association strives to keep the information accurate but cannot guarantee its completeness. The site may contain links to third-party sites for which the association is not responsible. Use of the site is at the user's sole risk.

sections.heading: Governing law and jurisdiction
sections.body: This notice and the use of the site are governed by Swiss law. Exclusive jurisdiction lies at the association's seat, subject to mandatory consumer-protection provisions.

terms.metaDescription: General terms of sale applicable to The Last Line services.
terms.title: Terms of sale
terms.intro: These general terms govern registrations and services offered by the non-profit sports association The Last Line through thelastline.ch.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.body: These general terms of sale (GTS) govern any registration for the services offered by the non-profit sports association The Last Line via thelastline.ch. By registering, the customer (parent or legal guardian, adult goalkeeper, club or association) accepts them without reservation. They prevail over any conflicting terms of the customer. The Last Line association, with its registered seat in Torgon, municipality of Vionnaz (Valais), is entered in the Business Identification Number (IDE/UID) register under CHE-235.374.703.

sections.heading: Services and plans
sections.body:
The association offers goalkeeper-specific training through the following plans:
Discovery session: single, no commitment, to try out our training.
Tour: a block of 18 sessions.
Full season: 36 sessions spread across the whole season.
Camps: occasional intensive training.
Details, session counts and prices are on the Offers page. Times and locations are shared through the member area and may be adjusted for organisational reasons.

sections.heading: Registration and account
sections.body: Registration is done online and requires creating an account. The customer warrants the accuracy of the information provided, in particular the identity, age and health of the registered goalkeeper. A minor is registered under the responsibility of their legal guardian.

sections.heading: Prices
sections.body: Prices are shown in Swiss francs (CHF) and are net. An automatic volume discount applies from the second goalkeeper registered in the same household. The association is not liable for VAT (turnover below the legal threshold): no VAT is added.

sections.heading: Payment terms
sections.body:
Discovery and single sessions: payable in full (100%) before the session.
Tour and full season: payment in one instalment (per tour or per season) or spread out (monthly or quarterly). Choosing a Tour or a season is a firm commitment for the entire package, regardless of the chosen payment method.
Payment is by Swiss QR invoice; card and TWINT payments (via Stripe) will be offered soon.
The place is definitively confirmed upon registration and its validation on the website.

sections.heading: Late payment and exclusion
sections.body: In the event of late payment, a reminder fee of CHF 10 is due from the 20th day after the due date. Failing settlement within 30 days of the due date, the association may exclude the goalkeeper, without refund: the total amount of the committed package (Tour or season) remains fully due. Default interest of 5% per year may be charged from default (art. 104 CO).

sections.heading: Commitment, cancellation and refund
sections.body:
Discovery and single sessions: non-refundable; they may only be rescheduled to a later date.
Tour and full season: packages are non-refundable, except in the event of the goalkeeper's injury duly certified by a medical certificate preventing participation in more than 50% of the sessions of the package concerned; in that case, a refund of 50% of the package price may be granted.
No other reason gives rise to a refund. Packages nevertheless remain freely transferable (see below).

sections.heading: Transfer and resale
sections.body: All plans (discovery session, Tour, full season) are freely transferable free of charge and resalable to a third party. A customer who cannot continue may thus transfer or resell their package to another goalkeeper. The transfer must be announced to the association beforehand; the beneficiary must meet the admission conditions (in particular age) and hold an account. The association charges no transfer fee.

sections.heading: Participant absences
sections.body: A session missed through the goalkeeper's own doing is neither refunded nor owed in addition. A make-up on another slot may be offered subject to availability, as a goodwill gesture and without guarantee or acquired right.

sections.heading: Sessions cancelled by the association, weather and force majeure
sections.body: The association controls neither weather conditions, nor the opening or closing of pitches, nor cases of force majeure. When a session cannot take place, the association endeavours to postpone or make it up. If a make-up proves impossible, the association is not liable and the amount provided remains due, without refund.

sections.heading: No right of withdrawal
sections.body: Under Swiss law, online registrations do not benefit from a legal right of withdrawal. The discovery session, with no commitment, lets you try our services before subscribing to a Tour or a season.

sections.heading: Insurance and liability
sections.body: Each participant takes part at their own risk and must hold personal liability and accident insurance. The association declines all liability for accidents, injury, loss or theft, save for its gross negligence. Instructions from the coaching staff must be followed.

sections.heading: Data protection
sections.body: Personal data processed in connection with registrations is described in our privacy policy, compliant with the nFADP and GDPR.

sections.heading: Changes to the GTS
sections.body: The association may amend these GTS. The applicable version is the one in force on the day of registration. The last-updated date is shown at the top of this page.

sections.heading: Governing law and jurisdiction
sections.body: These GTS are subject to Swiss law. Exclusive jurisdiction lies at the association's seat, in Torgon (Vionnaz, VS), subject to mandatory consumer-protection provisions.

privacy.metaDescription: Privacy policy compliant with the Swiss FADP and the European GDPR.
privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: The non-profit sports association The Last Line takes the protection of your data seriously. This policy explains what data we process, why, on what basis and what your rights are, in accordance with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP) and, where applicable, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

sections.heading: Data controller
sections.body: The data controller is the non-profit association The Last Line, whose seat is in Torgon (Vionnaz, VS). For any question about your data: contact@thelastline.ch.

sections.heading: Data we process
sections.body: Account: name, e-mail, phone, language. Registered goalkeepers (often minors): name, year/date of birth, level, dominant hand, any medical notes and photo (only with consent). Registrations and billing: chosen plan, sessions, amounts, invoices. Messages sent via the contact form. Strictly necessary technical data (login session, security).

sections.heading: Purposes
sections.body: Managing accounts and registrations, organising and following up sessions and camps, issuing invoices, communicating with you, keeping the site secure and preventing abuse. With your consent: sending news and using photos.

sections.heading: Legal bases
sections.body: Performance of the contract (registrations, services), your consent (photos, marketing, non-essential cookies), our legitimate interest (security, running the association) and compliance with legal obligations (accounting).

sections.heading: Processors and hosting
sections.body: We use providers acting on our behalf: Supabase (database hosting and authentication, data in the EU), Vercel (site hosting), LWS (e-mail), Cloudflare (anti-bot “Turnstile”) and, where relevant, Stripe (payments). These providers are contractually bound to protect your data. Some may be located outside Switzerland/EU; such transfers are covered by appropriate safeguards.

sections.heading: Retention
sections.body: We keep your data for as long as necessary for the above purposes and to meet our legal obligations (in particular accounting records, kept for 10 years). After these periods, data is deleted or anonymised.

sections.heading: Security
sections.body: We apply appropriate technical and organisational measures: encrypted connections (HTTPS), per-user access isolation (row level security), private storage of photos, restricted admin access and strong passwords.

sections.heading: Minors' data
sections.body: Minor goalkeepers are registered under the responsibility of their parent or legal guardian, who owns the account. No photo of a minor is used without the guardian's consent, which can be withdrawn at any time.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, object to and port your data, and to withdraw consent at any time. To exercise these rights: contact@thelastline.ch. You may also contact the competent supervisory authority: in Switzerland, the Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC); in the EU, your national data-protection authority.

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.body: The site uses only strictly necessary cookies and stores your consent choice. See our cookie policy for details.

sections.heading: Changes
sections.body: This policy may be updated. The last-updated date is shown at the top of this page.

cookies.metaDescription: Cookie usage policy for the The Last Line website.
cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.intro: The site thelastline.ch is published by the non-profit association The Last Line. This page explains which cookies the site uses. We use very few, and none for advertising or tracking.

sections.heading: What is a cookie?
sections.body: A cookie is a small file placed on your device by a website to make it work properly or to remember preferences.

sections.heading: Cookies we use
sections.body: Strictly necessary cookies: login session (via Supabase) that keeps you signed in and secures your area; storage of your cookie-consent choice; a temporary cookie for the booking flow. A technical cookie may also be set by Cloudflare Turnstile, used to tell humans from bots on our forms. These cookies are essential and are not used to track you.

sections.heading: No tracking or advertising cookies
sections.body: We use no Google Analytics, no advertising pixel and no third-party tracking tool. No data is sold or shared for advertising.

sections.heading: Managing cookies
sections.body: You can accept or decline via the banner shown on your first visit, and you can delete or block cookies at any time in your browser settings. Blocking strictly necessary cookies may prevent you from signing in.

OffersPage.metaDescription: Goalkeeper-specific training prices in the Chablais: discovery session, half-season (Tour) and full season, for youth (from age 10) and adults (18+). Club packages on quote.

hero.title: Clear pricing, for every keeper
hero.subtitle: Our season runs over 36 sessions, split into two Tours of 18 sessions each. Try a discovery session, commit to a Tour, or get the best rate with the full season.

single.title: Discovery session
single.description: A single session to discover the academy, with no commitment.

tour.title: Tour
tour.description: A commitment over a half-season: 18 sessions per Tour.

season.title: Full season
season.description: The whole season over 36 sessions, best value for money.

single.title: Discovery session
single.description: A single session to try our adult training, with no commitment.

tour.title: Tour
tour.description: A commitment over a half-season: 18 sessions per Tour.

season.title: Full season
season.description: The whole season over 36 sessions, best value for money.

club.title: Is your request more specific?
club.description: Get in touch for a quote, based on the number of goalkeepers and the frequency you want.

trial.question: Can I try before committing?
trial.answer: Yes. The discovery session (CHF 45 youth, CHF 50 adults) lets you try a full session with no commitment.

location.question: Where do the sessions take place?
location.answer: At the Terrain de la Mêlée in Aigle, in the heart of the Chablais (canton of Vaud), right by the motorway exit — central and easy to reach from across the Chablais and Valais. Changing rooms and showers available.

siblings.question: Is there a sibling discount?
siblings.answer: Yes, a discount is applied automatically from the second child enrolled on a Tour or Season plan.

payment.question: What payment methods do you accept?
payment.answer: Card and TWINT online via our platform, or Swiss QR-bill for bank transfers.

cancel.question: How does the commitment work?
cancel.answer: You commit by Tour (a half-season of 18 sessions) or by full season (36 sessions). At the end of a Tour, you're free to continue or not. The discovery session has no commitment at all.

club.question: How does the club contract work?
club.answer: Our Tour and Season plans are already suited to clubs and associations: simply enrol your goalkeepers. A volume discount is applied automatically based on the number of goalkeepers registered. For more specific needs, contact us for a tailor-made quote.

banner.text: Clubs, associations and parents can already choose their pack for the season. A volume discount is applied automatically based on the number of goalkeepers registered.

AcademyPage.metaDescription: Discover The Last Line: our mission, our coaching team, our pedagogical method and our facilities in the Chablais region.

hero.title: Goalkeeper specialists, from the ground up
hero.subtitle: An independent academy, built by goalkeepers for goalkeepers, in the heart of the Chablais region.
hero.imageAlt: View of the Dents du Midi from the Chablais

mission.title: Give every goalkeeper the tools to express themselves
mission.intro: The Last Line was born from a simple observation: in most clubs, goalkeepers receive little to no specific training. We fill that gap with a structured curriculum, specialized coaches and long-term follow-up.

team.title: Coaches who have stood in goal themselves
team.subtitle: All our coaches played the position at a competitive level and hold a national coaching certificate.

head.name: Gianluca Giannarelli
head.bio: Goalkeeping has always been his passion. He came up through Martigny-Sport, reached the first team and spent 5 years in LNB. After an injury and the club's relegation, he went on to defend the goal for FC Leytron, FC La Combe and FC Fully. He earned his Level 2 coaching diploma at Macolin. He also created the Giana Stop & Shoot tournament, to put goalkeepers in the spotlight at an event dedicated entirely to them.

youth.name: Arthur Chazelle
youth.bio: A goalkeeper since his earliest years, his development took off when he joined Dijon FCO at 16. He spent over four years there and broke into the professional squad, in Ligue 2 then Ligue 1. He also kept goal for FC Monthey, FC Aigle and Azzuri in the top amateur division. Passing the game on is in his DNA.

perf.name: Julien V.
perf.bio: Physical and mental preparation for goalkeepers in the performance pathway.

technical.title: Individual technique
technical.description: Stance, handling, diving, distribution. The fundamentals drilled and corrected in every session.

tactical.title: Reading the game
tactical.description: Positioning, communication with the defense, anticipation. The modern keeper plays with their feet and their head.

physical.title: Physical preparation
physical.description: Coordination, jumping, core strength, reaction speed. Tailored to each age group.

mental.title: Mindset & confidence
mental.description: Focus routines, error recovery, leadership. The most unforgiving position is also a mental one.

facilities.title: Our training venue
facilities.subtitle: In partnership with FC Aigle, one central, easy-to-reach site in the Chablais.

outdoor.title: La Mêlée pitch
outdoor.description: In partnership with FC Aigle, the 2026-2027 season trains on the La Mêlée pitch, right next to the motorway exit. A central spot in the Chablais that keeps everything simple to organise. A changing room and showers are available for extra comfort after training.

gear.title: Pro equipment
gear.description: Mobile goals, dummies, resistance bands, video. All specific gear is provided.

excellence.title: Caring excellence
excellence.description: We push high, we never humiliate. Every goalkeeper progresses at their own pace.

respect.title: Respect & fair play
respect.description: Of the opposing keeper, of the referee, of teammates. Sporting values come first.

passion.title: Love of the position
passion.description: Loving the goalkeeping role is the foundation. We feed that fire in every session.

recruit.title: We're looking for goalkeeper coaches
recruit.subtitle: Passionate about the position and keen to pass it on? Get in touch, we'd love to chat.

Inscription.metaDescription: Set up your registration in a few steps: profile, plan, goalkeepers and contact details.
Inscription.title: Book your season
Inscription.subtitle: Set up and save your registration in a few steps. You can then find it in your account.

step2.title: Your plan
step2.subtitle: Pick a plan for each goalkeeper. Add as many as you need.

private.label: Parent / Goalkeeper
private.desc: An individual sign-up or for your children.

club.label: Club or association
club.desc: For a club, an association or an organisation.

recap.note: Indicative price, excluding specific discounts. We confirm the final amount and payment details after receiving your request.

success.title: Registration saved!
success.body: Thank you! Your registration is saved. You can find it in your account under My invoices. Payment details will follow (or will be handled online once payments are enabled).
success.bodyCard: Thank you! Your registration is saved. The automatic charge is being set up; track your payments in My invoices.
success.bodyManual: Thank you! Your registration is saved. Pay your instalments from My invoices (TWINT or QR-invoice).

paid.title: Payment received!
paid.body: Thanks! Your registration is confirmed. You will receive a confirmation email. See you on the pitch!

auth.title: Sign in to finish
auth.body: To save your registration and track it in your account, sign in or create an account. Your entries stay here while you sign in in another tab.
auth.required: You must be signed in to complete the registration.
auth.bodyShort: An account is required to complete a registration.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.accountExists: An account already exists with this email.
errors.captcha: Anti-bot check failed. Please try again.
errors.confirm: Account created. Confirm your email to continue.

payment.title: Payment

twint.label: TWINT
twint.desc: You pay each instalment via TWINT.

payment.cardRecurringHint: Your card will be charged automatically on each instalment.
payment.twintHint: The 1st instalment is paid now via TWINT; the rest appear in My invoices.
payment.qrHint: One QR-invoice per instalment will be available in My invoices. You pay at your bank.
payment.oneTimeHint: Discovery session: a single payment, no instalments.

account.createSubtitle: An account is created with these details to track your registrations and invoices.

CookieBanner.message: We only use cookies that are strictly necessary for the site to work (login, security). No tracking or advertising cookies.
